laneType: EVAL (SFT/LoRA base-vs-adapter eval-ladder, per-item rows present)

adapterTrainConfig.note: THIS run's evaluated adapter is the 1-epoch uniform r=16 LoRA per hmwe3ahccd6vah.sophia_lora_config.json. It is NOT the 3-epoch --lora-rank-alloc adapter described in the bundled qwen2.5-3b-lora-3ep-rankalloc public-report (that report is TRANSCRIBED from an EARLIER, different run: run #9 / runId 28285622770). Do not attribute that run's 29/32 to this artifact.

evalSet.note: held-out provenance set; single-judge content+format scoring via tools/eval_local_model.py; deterministic FP16/greedy base

loadBearingMetric: passAt1 (content channel) — paired per-item base vs adapter
verifiedStepCoverageNote: N/A for this lane — provenance content-eval, not a process/step verifier. No VSC field exists in the artifact.

poweredVerdict.note: MDE 0.3502 > tolerance 0.05 at N=32: the probe CANNOT resolve this effect — grow N before claiming a direction

poweredVerdict_pairedRho0p5.note: even crediting per-item base/adapter correlation rho=0.5, MDE 0.2476 still >> the +0.0625 observed

mcnemar.note: discordant pairs 3 vs 5; p=0.72 — no significant directional shift

evalStats.interpretation: The +6.25pt (2/32) adapter content uplift is NOT statistically resolvable at N=32 single-judge: the 95% paired bootstrap CI [-0.125, +0.219] straddles zero and the probe's MDE (0.35, or 0.25 with rho=0.5) dwarfs the effect. Directional/candidate-only, consistent with eval noise.

harnessCheck.verdict: NOT a broken-verifier / 0-0 trap — base content passAt1 = 71.9% is well above zero and the verifier discriminates per item. This is a REAL (small, underpowered) eval, not a measurement artifact.
harnessCheck.firstRealPositiveReason: adapter > base (+6.25pt) BUT the 95% CI includes 0 and the result is underpowered (MDE >> delta). Does not meet the 'CI excludes 0' bar for a first real positive data point.
harnessCheck.templateTrap: Subject is a chat/instruct model (Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct), but it was scored via tools/eval_local_model.py and the FORMAT channel is high (base 84.4%, adapter 87.5%), indicating properly templated generation — NOT a raw-prompt template trap.
harnessCheck.rewardSpread: N/A — this is a deterministic single-generation pass/fail content eval, not a GRPO generation group, so there is no per-group reward spread to report.
harnessCheck.loadBearingNote: passAt1 (content) is load-bearing here, not meanReward; combined/format channels are format-driven and must not be read as content gains.

z3PromotionContext.caveat: hmwe3ahccd6vah.promotion.public-report.json is the W2 oracle bundle for candidate 'local-sophia-v2-mlx', whose metricRows are computed against an OFFLINE baseline ladder (total before 0.5 -> after 0.688), NOT against this GPU run's FP16 Qwen base (23/32). The z3 promote attests the invariant suite ran (protected_floor_content, no_total_regression, contamination_zero overlap=0, provenance_complete, solver_attestation all accepted) — it is NOT independent statistical evidence of the +6.25pt GPU uplift. Do not conflate.

registeredResultNote: This is a real-GPU RunPod (x86) eval, but it is NOT a registered headline result — it is candidate/directional only and underpowered. Tagging registeredResult=false.
overallVerdict: NO-GO (honest directional null). Real-GPU end-to-end path (GitHub Actions -> RunPod -> SFT/LoRA train -> eval-ladder -> z3 W2 oracle) is VERIFIED to run. The capability signal is NOT: base 71.9% content vs adapter 78.1% content = +6.25pt (2/32), 95% paired bootstrap CI [-0.125, +0.219] includes 0, underpowered (MDE 0.35 >> 0.05), McNemar p=0.72. Harness is healthy (base >> 0); this is a genuine small/underpowered eval, not a broken verifier. A validated claim needs the P6 protocol: >=3 seeds, 2 non-qwen judge families, kappa>=0.40, 95% CI excluding zero.

provenance.source: hmwe3ahccd6vah.eval_ladder_adapter.json (base/adapter rungs) + per-item content rows from hmwe3ahccd6vah.answers.tar.gz (benchmark/model_runs/local-qwen-qwen2.5-3b-instruct-*.report.json vs local-sophia-cuda-v1-*.report.json)
